summary: This notebook implements an inference pipeline for the BirdCLEF-2025 audio classification competition. It loads pre-trained EfficientNet-B0 models, converts raw audio files into mel spectrograms, and generates species presence predictions for 5-second time windows. The pipeline supports test-time augmentation via spectrogram flips and applies temporal smoothing to the final submission CSV to enforce consistency across adjacent segments. Readers should learn how to structure a production-ready inference script for audio tasks, handle multi-model averaging, and apply post-processing to improve leaderboard performance.
competition_flow: Loads pre-trained EfficientNet-B0 models and taxonomy data, processes test audio into mel spectrograms, runs inference with optional TTA and model averaging, generates a CSV submission, and applies temporal smoothing to the predictions.
data_reading: Loads taxonomy CSV to extract species labels.
Reads test audio files (.ogg) using librosa at 32kHz.
Reads sample submission CSV for column alignment.
data_processing: Computes mel spectrograms with configurable hop length and mel bins.
Applies TTA via horizontal and vertical flips of the spectrogram.
ensembling: Averages predictions across multiple loaded model checkpoints and across TTA variations (horizontal/vertical flips) before applying temporal smoothing.
insights: Test-time augmentation via spectrogram flips improves prediction robustness without additional training.
Temporal smoothing of adjacent 5-second windows enforces biological plausibility and boosts leaderboard scores.
Averaging outputs from multiple pre-trained checkpoints is a straightforward method to enhance inference performance.
